checks.finding: All 8 high-risk tasks touch sentinel surfaces named in risk-classification-policy.md (quality-gate decision inputs, public API contract, security chokepoint). T-006/T-007 medium and T-008 low are consistent with their Risk Rationale. No mismatch found.
All 8 Risk: high tasks carry both Red->Green evidence and independent-review-verdict-recorded Done When items. No critical-tier task exists.

Done When counts range 4-8 (T-001/T-004 at exactly 8, within threshold). Scope areas within 3; T-001's four sub-parts are one tightly-coupled bootstrap chokepoint module, not sequential independent phases. No oversizing or fragmentation.

Functional tasks map to error-path ACs (AC-002/003/004/012/014/016/017, cannot-parse fixtures for T-003/T-011). T-009's happy-path-only AC-013 is acceptable because resources are documented thin pass-through views whose error paths are inherited from upstream tasks' tests.

Unit tests target single modules; integration tests involve 2+ components; TEST-005 is correctly e2e over the full protocol workflow; TEST-011/016 match their static+runtime dual scope. No mismatched label.
All 8 high-risk tasks carry a Rollback: field specifying commit revert, several citing uninstall.sh --mcp sdd-forge-mcp, consistent with infra-spec.md. Medium/low tasks also carry Rollback fields.
No uncoordinated file overlap. T-010's next-command.ts replaces T-004's stub with proper Blockers ordering (create-then-replace). Test directories each owned by exactly one task.

Every Blockers edge verified against scope dependency; all 20 edges genuine, no spurious blocker, and no missing blocker that would cause integration failure (T-005's use of T-003's parser output is guaranteed transitively via T-004).
SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope; all 11 tasks are net-new feature implementation.
